sections.title: Rights to be treated with courtesy and compassion
sections.content: Victims have the right to be treated with courtesy and compassion, and respect for their dignity and privacy, by all persons exercising official functions in the administration of justice.

sections.title: Right to be informed of services
sections.content: Victims have the right to be informed, at the earliest practical opportunity, of the services available to them, including victim support services.

sections.title: Right to be informed of progress of investigation
sections.content: Victims have the right to be informed of the progress of the investigation of the offence, including whether a suspect has been arrested or charged.

sections.title: Right to be informed of prosecution and bail decisions
sections.content: Victims have the right to be informed of decisions regarding the prosecution of the offender, including decisions to modify or withdraw charges, and bail decisions affecting the offender.

sections.title: Right to give views on matters affecting victim
sections.content: Victims have the right to give their views, where practicable, on matters concerning the offence that affect them, including decisions regarding bail, pleas, and sentencing.

sections.title: Right to information about offender's release
sections.content: Victims have the right to be informed of the offender's release from custody, including on bail, temporary release, parole, or final release.

sections.title: Right to protection from offender
sections.content: Victims have the right to have their safety and security, and that of their family, considered in bail and sentencing decisions, and to have reasonable protection from the offender.

sections.title: Right to information in timely manner
sections.content: Victims have the right to be informed of their rights under this Act, and to receive information in a timely manner appropriate to the circumstances.

sections.title: Victim impact statement
sections.content: Victims have the right to make a victim impact statement to the court, describing the effects of the offence on them. The court must consider this statement in sentencing.

sections.title: Register of victims
sections.content: Victims may register to receive information about the offender's status, including release dates and conditions.
